questions.q: Alauddin Khalji belonged to which dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Alauddin was the most famous ruler of the Khalji dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: From which city did Alauddin Khalji rule his Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji ruled the Delhi Sultanate from its capital at Delhi.

questions.q: During which years did Alauddin Khalji rule Delhi?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji ruled from 1296 to 1316 CE.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji is especially famous for introducing what economic policy?
questions.explanation: Alauddin is known for his sweeping market reforms and price controls on essential goods.

questions.q: Which invaders did Alauddin Khalji repeatedly repel from the northwest?
questions.explanation: Alauddin successfully repelled several Mongol invasions of northern India.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji extended the Delhi Sultanate's power deep into which part of India?
questions.explanation: Alauddin extended the Sultanate deep into the south, launching campaigns into the Deccan and peninsular India.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji maintained what kind of military force?
questions.explanation: Alauddin built and maintained a large, powerful standing army directly paid by the state.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji's market reforms were chiefly intended to support what?
questions.explanation: By controlling prices, Alauddin could pay his huge army cash salaries while keeping costs low.

questions.q: Which famous Rajput fort did Alauddin Khalji besiege and capture around 1303 CE?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji besieged and captured the great Rajput fort of Chittor around 1303 CE.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji's reign falls within which broad period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: Alauddin ruled during the Delhi Sultanate period, which lasted from 1206 to 1526 CE.

questions.q: How did Alauddin Khalji come to the throne in 1296?
options: By killing his uncle and predecessor Jalaluddin
questions.explanation: Alauddin seized the throne by treacherously murdering his uncle and father-in-law, Sultan Jalaluddin Khalji.

questions.q: Which slave-general led Alauddin Khalji's most famous campaigns into the southern Deccan?
questions.explanation: Malik Kafur, Alauddin's trusted slave-general, led the celebrated expeditions into the Deccan and the far south.

questions.q: Which wealthy Gujarat kingdom did Alauddin Khalji conquer early in his reign around 1299?
options: The kingdom of Gujarat under the Vaghelas
questions.explanation: Alauddin's forces conquered the prosperous Vaghela kingdom of Gujarat around 1299.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji captured which Rajput fort in Rajasthan, famed for its later siege, besides Chittor?
questions.explanation: Alauddin captured the strong Rajput fort of Ranthambore in 1301 after a long siege.

questions.q: To pay his soldiers reliably, Alauddin Khalji enforced controlled prices in which key administered area?
options: The grain and cloth markets of Delhi
questions.explanation: Alauddin set up strictly regulated markets in Delhi controlling the prices of grain, cloth, and other essentials.

questions.q: The most serious Mongol attacks that Alauddin Khalji faced came from which empire?
questions.explanation: The Mongol invasions Alauddin repelled were launched by the Chagatai Khanate of Central Asia.

questions.q: Which southern kingdom of the Deccan, ruled by Ramachandra, did Alauddin's forces first raid and subjugate?
questions.explanation: Alauddin first attacked the Yadava kingdom of Devagiri under Ramachandra, even before becoming Sultan, and later subjugated it.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji introduced a system to record and pay soldiers that included branding horses. What was this measure called?
questions.explanation: Alauddin introduced the Dagh (branding of horses) and Chehra (descriptive roll of soldiers) to prevent fraud in the army.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji greatly increased state revenue by raising land tax on cultivators to what proportion of produce?
questions.explanation: Alauddin raised the land revenue demand to about half of the produce in the regions he directly controlled.

questions.q: Which grand title did Alauddin Khalji adopt, reflecting his ambition to be a world conqueror?
questions.explanation: Alauddin styled himself Sikander-i-Sani, the 'Second Alexander', expressing his ambitions of conquest.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji built which new fortified city and tank as part of his works in Delhi?
options: Siri and the Hauz Khas tank
questions.explanation: Alauddin founded the fortified city of Siri and constructed the great Hauz-i-Alai (Hauz Khas) reservoir.

questions.q: Who succeeded to power and intrigue after Alauddin Khalji's death in 1316, briefly dominating the court?
questions.explanation: After Alauddin's death in 1316, his general Malik Kafur briefly seized control before the dynasty collapsed.

questions.q: What was Alauddin Khalji's name before he assumed the throne and his regnal title?
questions.explanation: Alauddin's original name was Ali Gurshasp before he became Sultan and took the title Alauddin.

questions.q: Malik Kafur's campaigns reached as far south as which Pandya capital, the southernmost point of Khalji raids?
questions.explanation: Malik Kafur's expedition penetrated to Madurai, the Pandya capital, the deepest point of the Khalji raids into the south.

questions.q: Which Hoysala capital did Alauddin's forces under Malik Kafur attack during the southern campaigns?
questions.explanation: Malik Kafur attacked Dwarasamudra, the Hoysala capital, exacting tribute during the southern expeditions.

questions.q: The Kakatiya ruler of Warangal who submitted to Alauddin's forces, reportedly surrendering a great diamond, was whom?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiya king Prataparudra II of Warangal submitted to Malik Kafur, reportedly surrendering treasure including a famous diamond.

questions.q: Which decisive battle of 1306 saw Alauddin's general Ghazi Malik (Tughlaq) crush a major Mongol invasion?
questions.explanation: In 1306 Ghazi Malik decisively defeated the Mongols near the Ravi, one of the heavy blows that ended the Mongol threat under Alauddin.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji is said to have restricted nobles' gatherings and intermarriage for what reason?
questions.explanation: Alauddin curbed the nobles' wealth, social gatherings, and marriage alliances specifically to forestall plots against the throne.

questions.q: Which contemporary poet-historian, also linked to Balban's court, chronicled and praised Alauddin Khalji's reign?
questions.explanation: Amir Khusrau, the great poet, served at Alauddin's court and wrote works celebrating his conquests, while Barani later chronicled the reign.

questions.q: Alauddin Khalji is also credited with beginning which unfinished tower meant to rival the Qutb Minar?
questions.explanation: Alauddin began the Alai Minar, intended to surpass the Qutb Minar, but it was left unfinished after his death.
